issues.title: parser-failure-rate alert fires false-positive for degraded providers (NaN > threshold)
issues.description: Degraded/disabled scraper providers (nineanime, allanime, animefever, miruro, okru) have zero traffic. PromQL rate(errors)/rate(total) = 0/0 = NaN; Grafana evaluates NaN > 0.5 as true, causing false alerts every 30m. Fix: added traffic gate (same pattern as high-p95-latency):  — drops zero-traffic providers from result vector, noDataState: OK handles the rest.
issues.fix: Added traffic gate to parser-failure-rate PromQL in docker/grafana/provisioning/alerting/rules.yml (commit 088d6e00). Grafana restarted to pick up provisioned rule change.

Build cannot complete — esbuild Go subprocess crashes with `SIGABRT` / `EAGAIN` / `EPIPE` (`failed to load PostCSS config: EAGAIN`, `[vite:define] The service was stopped: write EPIPE`). Reproduces with `npm run build` and direct `node node_modules/vite/bin/vite.js build` — proves it is environmental, not bun-specific. dmesg shows recent OOM-kill activity in the user@0.service cgroup (3.77GB current usage); the host esbuild service keeps getting killed mid-transform.
